# Welcome to Parsley

> Learn about the Parsley library and its key features.

- [Welcome to Parsley](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/index.md): Welcome to Parsley
- [Quick Start](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/quick-start/index.md): Get started with Parsley by learning how to register and resolve services dynamically. This guide covers the basics of setting up service dependencies, implementing interfaces, and using Parsley’s service registry to structure your application with the Inversion of Control (IoC) design principle.
- [Licensing Information](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/licensing.md): The Parsley library is released under the Apache License, Version 2.0. This license allows you to freely use, modify, and distribute the library, provided that certain conditions are met, such as including the original license and a notice of modifications.
- [CLI Reference](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/index.md): With the Parsley CLI, you can generate boilerplate code for advanced DI features effortlessly Whether you're working with proxies, decorators, or need support for dynamic dependency resolution, Parsley CLI has you covered.

## Registration Concepts

- [Register Constructor Functions](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-constructor-functions.md): Learn how to register services in Parsley using constructor functions. Understand what constructor functions are in Go and how Parsley uses them to automatically resolve and inject dependencies into your application.
- [Register Factory Functions](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-factory-functions.md): Learn how to use factory functions in Parsley to gain more control over service creation. Discover the difference between factory and constructor functions, and see how factory functions enable dynamic service configuration with additional parameters.
- [Register Instances](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-instances.md): Learn how to use the RegisterInstance method in Parsley to manually register and manage complex service instances. Ensure consistent state and availability across your application by treating registered instances as singletons, making them accessible to other services as dependencies.
- [Register Service Modules](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-module.md): Learn how to group related services with the RegisterModule method in Parsley to maintain modular code structure. Discover how service modules improve code organization, reusability, and separation of concerns, making your applications easier to scale and manage.
- [Register Named Services](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-named-services.md): Learn how to use named service registration in Parsley for precise control over multiple service implementations. Discover how to associate unique names with services, configure different contexts, and resolve them dynamically to create a more flexible and adaptable application architecture.
- [Manage Multiple Service Implementations](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/registration/register-lists.md): Learn how to register and inject multiple services of the same contract type in Parsley using the RegisterList[T] method. Simplify service management and gain flexibility by automatically injecting arrays or slices of services that share a common interface or type.

## Resolving Services

- [Lifetime Scopes in Parsley](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/resolving/lifetime-scopes.md): Discover how to manage service instance lifetimes in Parsley through various lifetime scopes. This guide explains the significance of lifetime settings, detailing how they influence the frequency of constructor or factory method calls and the management of service instances in your application.
- [Passing Specific Instances to the Resolver](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/resolving/resolve-with.md): Explore how to use Parsley's ResolveWithOptions method to dynamically pass specific instances to the resolver, enhancing your application's flexibility in managing dependencies.
- [Live Services: Activating Unregistered Dependencies](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/resolving/resolve-live-services.md): Discover how to use the Activate method in Parsley to create instances of unregistered dependencies dynamically. This guide illustrates how to instantiate objects on-the-fly while leveraging registered services.
- [Lazy Proxies](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/resolving/resolve-lazy-proxy.md): Explore the concept of lazy proxies in Parsley, a feature that allows for the deferred creation of service instances until they are needed. This guide explains how lazy proxies can optimize performance by managing resource-intensive dependencies effectively.

## Advanced Features

- [Mocking Made Easy with Parsley](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/advanced-features/generate-mocks.md): This article introduces the new generate mocks CLI command in Parsley, a feature that simplifies the creation of mock implementations for service interfaces in Go.
- [Advanced Dependency Injection With Generated Proxies](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/advanced-features/generate-proxy-services.md): This article explores the advanced dependency injection capabilities offered by the Parsley CLI through its generate proxy command. You'll learn how to automatically generate proxy services and interfaces for your service contracts using //go:generate annotations.
- [Validating Service Registrations](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/advanced-features/service-registration-validation.md): This article discusses the importance of validating service registrations in Parsley using the built-in Validator service. You'll learn how the Validator helps identify critical issues like missing and circular dependencies, which can lead to runtime errors and application crashes.

## Integrations

- [Walkthrough: Parsley Integration with Cobra](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/integrations/cobra.md): Learn how to integrate Parsley with Cobra to build a modular and maintainable CLI application. This guide utilizes the cobra-extensions package to implement command handlers with typed services, showcasing how Parsley's dependency injection enhances your command-line projects.
- [Walkthrough: Parsley Integration with GoFiber](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/integrations/gofiber.md): Learn how to integrate the Parsley dependency injection framework with the GoFiber web framework. Follow this guide to set up a GoFiber application using Parsley, creating a more modular, testable, and maintainable codebase.

## Package Reference

### pkg

- [bootstrap](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/library/pkg/bootstrap.md): Official documentation of the Parsley´s "bootstrap" package
- [features](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/library/pkg/features.md): Official documentation of the Parsley´s "features" package
- [registration](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/library/pkg/registration.md): Official documentation of the Parsley´s "registration" package
- [resolving](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/library/pkg/resolving.md): Official documentation of the Parsley´s "resolving" package
- [types](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/library/pkg/types.md): Official documentation of the Parsley´s "types" package

## CLI Reference

### completion

- [completion](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/completion/index.md): Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
- [completion bash](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/completion/parsley-cli_completion_bash.md): Generate the autocompletion script for bash
- [completion fish](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/completion/parsley-cli_completion_fish.md): Generate the autocompletion script for fish
- [completion powershell](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/completion/parsley-cli_completion_powershell.md): Generate the autocompletion script for powershell
- [completion zsh](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/completion/parsley-cli_completion_zsh.md): Generate the autocompletion script for zsh

### generate

- [generate](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/generate/index.md): Generate boilerplate code for advanced DI features
- [generate mocks](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/generate/parsley-cli_generate_mocks.md): Generate configurable mocks for interface types.
- [generate proxy](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/generate/parsley-cli_generate_proxy.md): Generate generic proxy types for method call interception.
- [init](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/parsley-cli_init.md): Add Parsley to an application
- [markdown](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/parsley-cli_markdown.md): Exports Markdown documentation to the specified folder
- [version](https://matzefriedrich.github.io/parsley-docs/cli-reference/parsley-cli_version.md): Show the current Parsley CLI version
